FAQ

General

1. **Which memberships do I need?**

Team2Book is a software addon to Teamup Calendar.  

In order to use Team2Book, you need 2 separate memberships.

1) A TeamUp membership,  
2) A Team2Book membership.

You can find more information about the Teamup Calendar here: [https://www.teamup.com/](https://www.teamup.com/)

Teamup’s membership is sold based on the number of sub-calendars you need.    
You will need to add 1 sub-calendar for each :

1) Consumer (i.e. number of people in your team) for which you need to provide a resource.    
   1) 1 sub-calendar for each consumer \+   
   2) 1 sub-calendar for each consumer that wants to sync with his personal agenda/calendar.  
2) Resource (i.e. offices, lab equipment, etc) that you want to assign to consumers.  
3) A few extra sub-calendars (typically less than 5\) will be required for special needs. 

Note: Please note that the Free and Plus Teamup calendar membership will not be enough if you can to use special events.

The Team2Book’s membership you need depends only on:

1) The number of resources (i.e. offices, lab equipment, etc) that you want to assign to consumers.

Our [subscription calculator](https://team2book.com/subscriptions-calculator), which will help you decide on the best membership plan that suits your needs. Our calculator takes into account various factors such as the resources and consumers you have, a working sub-calendar, and any special events you may have. By entering this information into our subscription calculator, it will recommend the most suitable membership plan for you.

![][image1]

Generally, there are five [types of memberships](https://team2book.com/pricing): Free, Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise. 

![][image2]

1\. Free Membership: 

This is the basic plan that is usually offered for free. It provides limited access to Team2Book resources and features, such as a limited Number of Resources, doesn’t Supports Special Event and   Support Ticket, and limited Number of Custom Fields.

You can manage a maximum of 3 resources.  This plan is ideal for very small team or to try out Team2Book for free.

2\. Plus Membership: 

This plan offers more advanced features than the free plan, such as Includes Support Ticket, more number of Resources, however still doesn't support Special Events.

You can manage a maximum of 6 resources.

3\. Pro Membership: 

The Pro plan is designed for users who need even more advanced features and functionality. It typically includes all the features of the Plus plan but with additional benefits such as Supports Special Events, more Number of Custom Fields, and more Number of Custom Field Options.

You can manage a maximum of 15 resources.

4\. Business Membership: 

This plan is designed for businesses that need to manage resources or teams simultaneously. It includes all the features of the Pro plan but with additional benefits such as more Number of Consumers that can be assigned to special events, more Number of Custom Fields, more Number of Custom Field Options, and more Number of Special Event Types. 

You can manage a maximum of 30 resources.

5\. Enterprise Membership: 

The Enterprise plan is designed for large organizations with complex resource management needs. It includes all the features of the Business plan but with additional benefits such as more Number of Consumers that can be assigned to special events, more Number of Custom Fields, more Number of Custom Field Options, and more Number of Special Event Types.

You can manage a maximum of 50 resources.  Please contact-us for more.

To know more about the Teamup Calendar plan, please visit: [https://www.teamup.com/pricing/](https://www.teamup.com/pricing/)

2. **Why do I need a separate Team2Book and TeamUp membership?**

Because,  we didn't actually reinvent the wheel when it comes to scheduling. Instead, we rely on the power of [Teamup Calendar](https://www.teamup.com/), a collaborative calendar software and mobile app that is used by millions of people worldwide. Team2Book and Teamup are not affiliated, but both software work seamlessly together to enhance your scheduling and booking experience. To use Team2Book, you must first have a Teamup account. If you don't have one yet, you can sign up at [teamup.com](https://www.teamup.com/). Once you have a Teamup account, you can then add the Team2Book add-on to your account. Please note that you need a separate membership for both Team2Book and TeamUp to use the features of the former. However, we believe that the investment is worth it as it will help streamline your scheduling and booking processes. With Teamup, all you need to do is enter your availability and let our innovative scheduler build your organization's schedule. It's that simple\! By leveraging the power of this proven tool, we can move faster and reduce our cost and provide you with an innovative scheduling tool.  We're proud to use Teamup Calendar as part of our workflow, and we think you'll love it too.

3. **What type of support do you offer?**

Team2Book offers several types of support, including: 

1\. Efficient Resource Allocation: 

Team2Book assigns consumers to resources based on their availability, preferences, and priority. This ensures that resources are utilized optimally and that consumers get the best possible service. 

2\. Real-Time Scheduling: 

Team2Book provides real-time scheduling updates, allowing users to view the availability of resources and make changes accordingly. 

3\. Automated Notifications: 

Team2Book sends automated notifications to user’s when a new appointment is scheduled or an existing one is rescheduled or canceled, and in case of scheduling problems.

4\. Customizable Settings: 

Team2Book allows users to customize settings such as working hours, time zones, and appointment durations to suit their specific needs. 

5\. Safety

Prevent office double-booking.

5\. Cost Effective 

Helps to maximize your current assets before moving to a bigger office. Reduce your price per square foot\!

7\. Reporting and Analytics: 

Team2Book provides detailed reports and analytics on resource utilization, consumer and resource scheduling, and other key metrics that help businesses make informed decisions.

4. **How can this product help us?**

The Team2Book product can help you in several ways. Here are some of the benefits: 

1\. Saves Time: 

Team2Book automates the scheduling process, which saves time for the manager. The Team’s manager does not have to spend hours manually scheduling resources, which can be a tedious and time-consuming task. 

2\. Increases Efficiency: 

Team2Book optimizes the use of resources by automatically scheduling them based on availability and priorities and preferences. This increases efficiency and reduces waste. 

3\. Improves Accuracy: 

Team2Book eliminates errors that may occur during manual scheduling, such as double bookings or conflicts. This improves accuracy and ensures that resources are used effectively. 

4\. Enhances Collaboration: 

Team2Book allows multiple users to access and update schedules in real-time, which enhances collaboration and communication among team members. 

5\. Provides Insights: 

Team2Book provides insights into resource utilization, availability, and priorities and preferences, which helps the user make informed decisions about resource and consumer allocation. 

6\. Streamlines Operations: 

Team2Book streamlines operations by automating repetitive tasks, reducing manual intervention, and improving overall productivity. In summary, Team2Book  can help the user save time, increase efficiency, improve accuracy, enhance collaboration, provide insights, and streamline operations.

5. **What are the 4 types of Team2Book’s users?**

The four types of users in Team2Book are: Principal Manager, Client Manager, Consumer, and Viewer (Guest user). 

1\. Principal Manager: 

This user is responsible for managing the overall operations of the Team2Book product. A Principal Client Manager account is created while setting up your Team2Book account. The primary difference between a Principal Client Manager and a Client Manager in Team2Book is the level of privilege. A Principal Client Manager can add and remove other client managers and can manage the plan.

2\. Client Manager: 

A Client Manager in Team2Book is responsible for managing the resources and consumers (preferences, consumers and resources, etc.) of a company or organization. They are responsible for overseeing the allocation of resources, such as office, lab equipment or anything needed for successful management and completion of a task.   The client manager receives all the notifications related to resource allocation problems and has write access to all the consumers’ sub-calendar.  Finally, the client manager has access to the Team2Book’s client dashboard in order to change the organization’s configuration.

3\. Consumer: 

Consumer is a user that needs to use or consumes a resource. This user is a consumer who has been assigned a resource by their Client Manager.   The consumer will use Teamup to enter availability or non-availability and to see the resource allocations.

4\. Viewer (Guest user): 

This user is a guest who has limited access to the platform's features and functionalities. They can view and will have read access to the resources sub-calendars but don’t need to consume any resources.

6. **What is a consumer?**

 A consumer is any individual who uses or utilizes a resource, and any one who wants to be assigned a specific available resource.  They can be staff members, individuals or employees of an organization. E.g. Employees like health-care professionals in a clinic that needs to use offices, which is a resource.

![][image3]

For more info [click here](https://team2book.com/category/tutorials)

7. **What is a resource?**  
   

Resource is used to refer to any kind of space or material that needs to be scheduled and allocated to its consumers. Resources can be an office, a premise, a lab equipment or anything that needs to be shared efficiently for your operations.  The resources are listed here: Client Dashboard \-\> Resources.  
![][image4]

For more info [click here.](https://team2book.com/tutorials/resource-category-and-resource-module)

8. **What is a constraint?**  
   

Constraint is the minimum number of available consumers required per or on a given time duration.  The client manager will get notified every time a constraint is not met.  The constraint violations are also displayed in the Scheduler Section of the Client Dashboard.

![][image5]

For more info [click here](https://team2book.com/category/tutorials)

9. **What is a priority?**

The priority is the level of importance assigned to a consumer by the client manager, which determines their access to reserved resources. Consumers with a priority of 1 have the highest priority and are given first access to resources, while consumers with priorities between 2 and 10 have no reserved resources but may have resource preferences. The scheduler schedules available resources based on consumer priority and preferences.

![][image6]

For more info [click here](https://team2book.com/category/tutorials)

10. **What are preferences?**

Resource preference refers to the process of selecting a preferred resource. A consumer with priority 1 has no preferences.  He has a reserved resource.  A consumer with priority 2 to 10 does not have a reserved resource.  Therefore for these consumers, we use preferences to assign a resource.  The scheduler will first try to assign a consumer his preferred resource if available, if not the scheduler will try to assign the consumer his second preferred resource and so on.  A consumer with a set priority of 2 to 10, will display the resource preference fields. It is important to note the same resource name cannot be chosen more than once for a given consumer.

![][image7]

For more info [click here](https://team2book.com/category/tutorials)

11. **What is a reserved resource?**

The reserved resource refers to a specific resource that is set aside or allocated for a particular purpose or user. When priority number 1 is selected, only the reserved resource will be shown, indicating that it is exclusively available for use by the consumer who has made the reservation.  When a consumer with a reserved resource is available to work, automatically he will be assigned this resource.  If the consumer is not available, his reserved resource will be made available to other consumers.

![][image8]  
For more info [click here](https://team2book.com/category/tutorials)

12. **Can I use my mobile device?**  
    

Yes, Team2Book is now accessible and can be used through your mobile phone. We have developed a mobile-friendly version of our product that allows you to access it anytime, anywhere. Whether you are on the go or simply prefer using your mobile phone over a desktop computer, our product is now easily accessible through your smartphone. You can now enjoy all the features and benefits of our product with just a few taps on your screen. Our team has worked hard to ensure that the mobile version of our product is user-friendly and easy to navigate. You can expect the same high-quality experience as you would on a desktop computer. 

To access our product through your mobile phone, simply visit our website using your smartphone's web browser.  Our website will automatically adjust to fit the size of your screen, making it easy for you to use and navigate

Teamup Calendar offers a Mobile that can be installed on users’ mobile devices.  It is available for both iOS (Apple) and Android devices.   Many users will prefer to install and use the Teamup’s Mobile app to enter availability/non-availability and see the resource allocation.

13. How do I submit a feature request?  
      
14. **We build our schedule not every month but every 2-6 months.  How can we use Team2Book?**

We understand that everyone's needs are different, which is why we have designed our Number of months to be scheduled as flexible and customizable. With Team2Book, you can now schedule your resource and consumer every 2-6 months according to your company needs. Our client detail dashboard allows you to enter the number of months you wish to schedule at a time, making it easier for you to plan ahead and stay organized. To set the number of months for scheduling, simply access the client dashboard and enter the desired number manually or use the arrow up/down feature. This flexibility ensures that you can customize your scheduling preferences based on your unique business requirements.

![][image9]

Team2Book is designed as a monthly tool.  Which means every month we schedule an additional month (i.e. we assign resources to consumers for one more month).  Every month, we will ask consumers to enter their availability or non-availability.  That said, we have added a few features that can help you schedule on a X month basis.  

For example:

- You can specify additional Months to Schedule Special Events.  

![][image10]

- You can run the special event assigner manually when ready.  
- You can send the enter availability and non-availability notification and the reminder manually when you are ready.  

With these added features, we have customers that successfully used Team2Book to help them build schedules and assigned resources with a X month interval.  

15. **Can I use Team2Book to build my employees’ work schedule?**

1) **Team2Book is an availability scheduling tool**.  Team2Book can assign available resources to available consumers based on the organization and consumers priority and preferences.  
2) **Team2Book is a booking tool**.  You can book a specific resource on a specific date and time.  
3) **Team2Book is an employee scheduling tool.**  Using what we call special events, the manager can decide who is working when and where.  The assigner can be configured to help you build your employees’ work schedule faster in a more automated manner.

16. **I have team’s member sometimes working from home.  Can I use Team2Book to schedule home office resources?**

Yes, If you have team members who work from home you can use Team2Book to schedule home office resources.  The same way you create a resource for each of your on-site office rooms, you can create remote resources.  Team2Book will assign these resources to consumers based on the consumer’s preferences the client manager defines.  

For example, you can create a remote office resource and decide that if there is no more office in-house, you will assign this remote office as a last resort.

17. **How can I see the summary of my account usage?**

You can easily check your account usage summary by accessing the client dashboard. Once you log in, simply click on the "Subscription" tab, and you will be able to view your Team2Book and TeamUp subscription status. This feature allows you to keep track of your account usage.  
![][image11]

![][image12]

![][image13]

18. **Can I add modifications directly in the Teamup user interface?**

Unfortunately, it is not recommended to make modifications directly in the Teamup user interface as it can create errors in the scheduler. This can result in scheduling conflicts and other issues that may negatively impact your team's data (preferences, consumers and resources, etc.) in Team2Book. 

The Teamup User Interface should only be used to view/enter/remove events, to read the working sub-calendars .ics, to setup an administrative access link and to link to manage your Teamup account.  Everything other configuration needs to be done using the Team2Book Client Dashboard.

19. What is availability scheduling?  
      
20. **How can I reset a user password?**

You can reset the user password. To reset your password, please follow the instructions below:  
   
1\. Log in to your Team2Book account with your Client Manager credentials. 

2\. In the homepage of Team2Book, tab the ***Members Area***.

3\. Select **Client Dashboard**.

![][image14]  
4\. Navigate to the user (Consumer, Viewer, Client Manager) section of the client dashboard. 

![][image15]

4\. Locate the user whose password needs to be reset. 

5\. Click on the "Update" button  next to their name. 

![][image16]

6\. Click on the "Reset Password" button

![][image17]

7\. Follow the prompts and enter a new password 

8\. Click submit 

![][image18]

21. **How can I find a consumer availability/non-availability sub-calendar link?**

The consumer availability/non-availability sub-calendar link can be easily found on the consumer dashboard. All you need to do is click on the update button for the consumer you want, and then scroll down to the bottom of the new prompt page. There, you will find a calendar link which leads to the consumer availability/non-availability sub-calendar. Using your right mouse button, you can copy the link and send it by email to the consumer that forgot it.  

![][image19]

22. **How can I resend the consumer welcome email?**

Unfortunately, we do not have a way to resend the welcome message once it has been sent. However, we suggest that you allow the welcome message radio button when creating a user account for the first time. This will ensure that all new users receive the welcome message upon sign-up.

![][image20]  
You can use the reset password feature to send the consumer his sub-calendar link.  

**Account  management** 

1. **I have a coupon code where do I redeem it?**

To redeem your coupon code, please follow these simple steps: 

1\. Log in to your Team2Book dashboard.   
2\. Click on the "Plan" tab.   
3\. Select "Membership" from the drop-down menu.   
4\. Proceed to the check-out page. [https://team2book.com/checkout](https://team2book.com/checkout)  
5\. Click on the link ‘Click here to enter your code’ on the top of your screen.  
6\. Enter your coupon code in the designated field and click "Redeem." 

Once you have successfully redeemed your coupon code, you will receive a confirmation message on screen and your discount will be applied to your purchase.

![][image21]

2. **How can I cancel my subscription?**

You can cancel your subscription at any time from the ‘My Account’ page.  Note: Only the Principal Client Manager can cancel the subscription plan. To cancel your subscription, You need to follow the following steps: 

1\. Go to the Members area   
2\. Click on My Account   
3\. For the left menu select ‘My Subscription’  
4\. Click the Cancel button on the right.  
![][image22]

. O

3. **Can I upgrade or downgrade my membership?**

Yes,  you have the flexibility to upgrade or downgrade your membership plan at any time. To do so, simply log in to your Team2Book account and click Members Area and navigate to My Account. From there, click on the My subscription tab and you will be able to select the option to upgrade or downgrade your membership plan.

![][image23]

You can click on the "Upgrade" or "Downgrade" button.  
 ![][image24]

And you will be redirected to a new subscription page where you can select the new membership plan that suits your needs.

![][image25]  
Please note that you will be credited for what you already paid, for any upgrades.  After pressing the ‘Switch subscription button’, you will be redirected to the payment page where you can see the details.

No refunds are provided for downgrades.  However, downgrading your plan will make sure that you will only be charged for what you need on the next renewal date.

**Setup**

1. **I purchased my Teamup and Team2Book membership.  How do I get started?**

To get started you need to create an Admin access key in Teamup. The Admin Access key is a unique identifier that will allow you to complete the client setup process. Without this key, you will not be able to proceed with the setup. Once you have created the Admin Access key, please make sure to keep it safe and secure.  This Access Key enables Team2Book access to your Teamup account to automatically create/delete/modify sub-calendars, events, custom fields, about section, etc.  

To get and enter your Teamup Admin key into Teamup Calendar in the client set-up section. Firstly, please log in to your Teamup account. Once you have logged in, go to settings and select Sharing. From there, click Create link and enter a name for the link. Then, select the Administration yes radio button and click save. After completing these steps, you will be directed to a sharing page. Please copy the link provided on this page and enter it into the Teamup calendar in the client set-up section.

To help you with this process, we would like to direct your attention to a video tutorial that provides step-by-step instructions on how to obtain and enter your Teamup Admin key into the client set-up section of the calendar. The video is available at [https://team2book.com/tutorials/video](https://team2book.com/tutorials/video) 

To complete the client setup process, you will need to gather a list of consumers and their emails, resources, and provide generic information about your organization. This information will be used to customize your Teamup account according to your needs. Please note that all the information entered during the client setup process can be changed later from the Client Dashboard. The Client Dashboard will only be visible once the client setup is completed.

2. **How can I change the number of months to schedule?**

You have the ability to change the number of months to schedule in advance through our members area. To do so, simply login and navigate to the client dashboard tab. From there, you can adjust the number of months to schedule at a time based on your specific needs. We understand that everyone's scheduling preferences may differ, and we want to ensure that our platform is customizable to meet your unique requirements.

![][image26]

3. **Why do we need a schedule offset day?**

As you may know, our system requires consumers to enter their availability and non-availability in their sub-calendar before starting the scheduler schedule for the next month. This is to ensure that the scheduler can accurately assign resources based on the consumer's availability. 

The schedule offset day aims to provide enough time to all consumers to enter their availability or non-availability before the next month's scheduling begins.  Scheduling the next month too early will provide inaccurate resource assignments and will trigger too many scheduling problem notifications sent to the client manager.

The scheduling offset day is the day of the month when we will start scheduling the next month.  It should be after the "Reminder Notification Day" to give consumers enough time to enter their availability and non-availability in their sub-calendar. 

4. **How can I change the scheduling offset day?**

Changing the scheduling offset day in your Team2Book account is a simple process. All you need to do is log in to your account and navigate to the Members area. From there, click on the Client dashboard and you will be directed to your client details. In the client details section, you can easily set the scheduling offset day according to your preference. This will allow you to adjust your schedule and It allows consumers to have enough time to enter their availability and make any necessary adjustments to their sub-calendar before the next month begins.

![][image27]

5. **How can I change the enter availability notification days?**

You have the ability to easily change the day of your monthly notification for consumers to fill their availability/non-availability sub-calendar.

To do so, simply log in to your Team2Book account and navigate to the Members area. From there, click on the Client dashboard and you will find your client details. In the client details section, you can set the notification day as per your preference.

![][image28]

You can also change the Reminder Notification Day.  This is the day of the month the reminder notification will be sent.  
![][image29]

6. **How can I change the enter availability notification message content?**

You have the ability to change the content of your enter availability notification message on Team2Book. This means that you can customize the message to suit your specific needs and preferences. To make changes to your notification message, simply log in to your Team2Book account and navigate to the Members Area. From there, click on the client dashboard and select the Notification tab. You will then see a list of notifications messages, including your enter availability notification. Click on the Update button and you will be taken to a section where you can update the description. 

![][image30]  
![][image31]

Here, you can customize the message content as per your requirements. There is a dynamic variable at the bottom of this email message content that holds a specific target. This variable can be used as per your requirement. The dynamic variable has been included to provide you with more flexibility and control over the email content. You can use it to personalize the message, add specific information, or tailor it to suit your needs.

![][image32]

Once you have made your changes, simply click on the Submit button and your new notification message will be saved.

7. **What is the access level of the principal client manager?**

Principal client managers have the authority to perform various tasks related to managing their Team2Book account. Firstly, they have the ability to remove and add client managers as per their requirements. Also a Principal Client Manager can clear all client manager data in the client dashboard.  This ensures that you have complete control over who has access to their account. Secondly, principal client managers can cancel the membership plan if needed. Meaning the principal client manager can also manage the subscription. This may be required in case of any changes in their business requirements or if they wish to switch to a different plan. Thirdly, they can set up the client details on their behalf. Lastly, principal client managers can upgrade or downgrade the membership plan based on their needs. 

The Principal client manager has all the access the client managers have (see below).

![][image33]

8. **How do I add another client manager?**

To add a client manager, navigate to the members area and click on the client dashboard tab. From there, Select the Client Managers and click on the Add New button. Enter all necessary requirements for the new client managers and click on the Submit button to confirm and save the information.

![][image34]

Having a second client manager provides redundancy in case one of them is unavailable or unable to manage the team's data (preferences, consumers and resources, etc.) in Team2Book. This means that if one of the client managers is sick or on vacation, there will always be someone else who can step in and take over their responsibilities. This ensures that there are no interruptions or delays in managing the team's data, which is crucial for ensuring that everything runs smoothly. Secondly, having a second client manager in charge of the same account ensures that assigning consumer events in Team2Book are addressed in a timely manner. With two people managing the account, there will always be someone available to respond to any requests or issues that arise. This means that any problems can be resolved quickly and efficiently, which is essential for maintaining good relationships with consumers.

9. **What are the accesses of the client manager?**

The role of a client manager is to manage the team's data such as preferences, consumers, resources, and special events in Team2Book. They have the privilege to assign consumers and special events in Team2Book. However, client managers cannot have the privilege to clear all other client manager data in the client dashboard. Client managers are not allowed to edit or update other client manager data in the client manager table as well. Additionally, they do not have the privilege to cancel the membership plan or upgrade/downgrade the membership plan.

By default, Client Managers can add, modify and delete Teamup Events for the following sub-calendars.  

- All the Consumer availability sub-calendars  
- Special Events sub-calendar  
- Closed Days sub-calendar.

![][image35]

10. **What are the accesses of a viewer?**

Viewers are users who have read-only access to a Teamup resource system. This means that they can view the sub-calendars or resource events, but they cannot make any changes or add any content. Viewers can be given access to specific sub-calendars or all sub-calendars in the system, depending on their role and responsibilities. It is important to note that viewers do not have availability/non-availability calendars like consumers, and they are not considered as the consumers of the resource event. For example, in a medical clinic setting, receptionists may be assigned as viewers to let the rest of the team know where they are working and to inform patients about where their healthcare professional is working.

11. **How do I add a viewer?**

To add a viewer login to your Team2book account, navigate to the members area, then click the client dashboard, then tab to Viewers, then click add new button. From there, enter the necessary information and click the Submit button to confirm and save the information.

![][image36]

12. **Why are there still free resources but the consumer was not assigned a resource?**

There will be free resources available on our platform that are not assigned to any consumer. This is because our scheduler schedules resources based on the preferences of each individual consumer. If a resource is not on the list of consumer preferences, it will not be assigned to this consumer and it will be considered as a free resource. To access these free resources, you can check the scheduler calendars and click Free resource sub-calendar. This will allow you to view all available resources, including those that have not been assigned to any consumer.

![][image37]

Here is a summary of why a consumer does not get assigned to a resource even though there are free resources available.

* The resource is not part of the consumer's resource preferences.  
* The resource is not available to cover the entire event.  For example, if Resource 1 opening hours are from 8h30 to 16h30 and the consumer event is from 8h to 16h30, the resource will not be assigned to the consumer.  The resource is not available from 8h to 8h30.  In this example, you can adjust the resource opening and closing time or adjust the consumer availability event start and close time.  
* There is a resource available in the morning and a different resource available in the afternoon.  If the consumer cannot be splitted (a parameter you can activate from Team2Book Dashboard-\>Consumers), then the scheduler will not try to assign the consumer 2 different resources for the same day.

13. **What business opening and closing hours should I use??**

Team2Book is designed to cater to the specific business hours of your organization, with adjustable opening and closing times. Throughout the day, clients may designate specific times that they will be open and closed. 

You should enter what is your normal opening and closing hours for your organization.

When creating an ‘All-Day’ event in Teamup, consumers will be considered available or non-available from the start time to the end time. Furthermore, each resource may have its own set of opening and closing times. For example, a computer lab might only be available in the afternoon on Wednesdays, even if the school is open all day. This level of customization ensures that Team2Book can accommodate even the most complex scheduling needs. 

![][image38]

The default opening time of business in Team2Book is 08:30. Additionally, the default closing time of business is 21:00. This means that your services will be available until this time. To enter your desired time, you can either input it directly or use the up and down arrows for precision.

For example, a medical clinic might be open from 8 to 17h even though half of the staff is working from 8h30 to 16h30.  Some doctors may offer walk-in clinics 2 nights per week and on weekends but regular staff are not working at night or weekends.  With this example, we recommend setting your opening and closing time from 8h to 17h on weekdays and enabling the parameter ‘Schedule Special Events on Closed Days’.  This will avoid regular staff to be assigned a resource from 8h to 21h but still allow special evening and weekends shifts.

14. **How to Enable Consumers to rotate resources?**

Team2Book platform allows for the rotation of resources to better suit consumer needs. This is to make sure that consumers are not always assigned the same resource.  

There are certain conditions that must be met in order to enable this feature.

Firstly, the priority of the consumer must be greater than 1\. To do this, simply go to your client dashboard and click on the "Consumer" tab. From there, you can adjust the priority level accordingly.

![][image39]

Once this is done, a "Resource Preferences Rotation" option will appear. Here, you can assign a consumer to rotate a resource per day, per week or per month as per your requirements.

![][image40]

It is important to note that the rotation direction is left; meaning that the second resource preference becomes the first and the first resource preference becomes the last preference.

There are also different rotation options available: No Rotation (resource preferences will not change), Per Day (resource preferences orders will be rotated on each day of the week), Per Week (resource preferences order will be rotated on each week of a month) and Per Month (resource preferences orders will be rotated on each month of the year).

It is the consumers’ priority, availability and preferences that influence which resource is assigned to a given consumer.   The feature above rotates the consumer’s resources preferences order and does not guarantee that the consumer will be assigned his preferred resource. 

15. **Do I get notified if there are not enough consumers to meet a constraint?**

YES, you will receive a notification if there are not enough consumers to meet a constraint. To receive this notification, please follow these steps:

1\. Log in to your client dashboard.  
2\. Go to the Notification section.  
3\. Click on User Enabled Notifications.  
4\. Turn on the toggle for “Insufficient Number of Consumers”.  
5\. Click Save

By doing so, you will be notified if there are not enough consumers to meet a constraint.

![][image41]

Additionally, if you turn on the toggle for Global Notification, you will also receive notifications for other important updates. 

16. **How can I prevent last minute changes?**

To prevent last minute change to your resource and consumer scheduling, simply click on the Client Dashboard and navigate to the Client Detail section.

Once there, you can set the Number of Days to Freeze Resource Events by entering any number starting from 1\. By setting this value to 7, for example, you will prevent any modifications to your resource scheduling for the upcoming 7 days. Alternatively, if you wish to allow last-minute changes, you can set this value to 0\.

![][image42]

Please be sure to click “Save” after making any changes to the “Number of Days to Freeze Resource Events” in the client detail.

By utilizing this feature, you can ensure that your resource scheduling remains consistent and reliable without any unexpected surprises or disruptions.

If an event is added/removed or updated that will affect the scheduling during this period, the Client Managers will be notified and can decide to approve the requested changes.  This is useful if you need to be informed that a consumer is updating his/her schedule at the last minute so that you can take appropriate action.  For example a medical Clinic will want to use this feature to know that a healthcare professional will not be available so that the secretary can cancel booked appointments.

17. **How to optimize resource usage?**

Sometimes, there is no resource available for a consumer event duration but there are 2 resources partly available to cover the consumer event duration.

By allowing events to be splitted, you can indicate to the scheduler that it is ok for this consumer to assign 2 partly available resources on the same day.  This can be done by splitting the event in half, with one half taking place before lunchtime and the other half taking place after lunchtime.

As a client manager, you have the ability to configure the split time and authorize event splitting for your consumers. When you select the "Allow events to be splitted" option in the Consumer section of your client dashboard and click Save, our scheduler will try to find time availability for both halves of the consumer event.

![][image43]

It's important to note that while event splitting can further optimize resource usage, it does require that consumers accept the use of different resources on a single day.  The scheduler will always try to find a resource available for the whole event duration first before trying to optimize and split events.

18. **How can I select who receives notifications?**

To select who receives notifications, please follow these simple steps. First, go to the members area and click on the client dashboard. Then, navigate to the Notification tab and click on User Enabled Notifications. From here, you can select who receives the notification by turning on the toggle for the receiver user. Finally, select who receives the notifications by turning on their toggle switch. Click Save after your selection. 

![][image44]

You will see rows with a list of users who can receive notifications and in the column, there are types of notifications that you can allow by turning on the toggle. 

By default, consumers receive the ‘Enter Availability’,  ‘Enter Availability Reminder notification’, and the welcome notification.  The Client managers received all the notification.  If the Global Notification toggle switch is turned on, all the notifications will be sent to this consumer.

19. **How many resource preferences should I set?**

There is no limit to select resource preferences. The selection of resource preferences is entirely based on the consumer's choice and the availability of resources. Therefore, you can set any number of resource preferences as per your requirement.

We recommend that you set as many resource preferences as possible.  If a resource is not in the consumer’s preferences list, that consumer will not be assigned to this free resource.

![][image45]

20. **What is the reason for utilizing categories to classify resources and consumers?** 

You can add resource categories.  For Example:

![][image46]

By adding categories, you can better group Team-Up sub-calendars.

![][image47]

You can get specific resources usage statistics for each Resource Category:

![][image48]

Similarly, you can add consumer roles.  For example:

![][image49]

By adding roles, you can better group your consumers’ Team-Up sub-calendars.

![][image50]

You can get specific statistics for each consumer roles:

![][image51]

You can also add constraints for specific consumers roles:

![][image52]

21. **How do I make a resource available for only part of the day or part of the week?**

To make a resource available for only part of the day or part of the week, follow these steps: 

This can be easily done by accessing your client dashboard and navigating to the "Resource" tab. From there, simply select the resource you wish to modify and set the hours of operation as desired.

![][image53]

To make a resource available for only part of the day,  Select the opening hours of the day that you want to make the resource available for, then fill the closing time to that you want to make the resource availability ends.

For example, if you set the opening hours of your resource center to 08:30 and the closing time to 12:30 on Monday. This means that the resource center will only be available during this time.

![][image54]

To make a resource available for only part of the week,  Select the days of the week that you want to make the resource available for and set hours of operation, then fill the other days of the week opening and closing time to 00:00. 

For example, if you set hours of operation for the resource availability on Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday and fill the other days of the week to 00:00, the resource will only be available on Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday.

This means that if anyone needs to access the resource on any other day of the week apart from Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday, they will not be able to do so.   
 

![][image55]

**Sub-calendars**

1. **What is an availability sub-calendar?**

The availability sub-calendar is a type of consumer sub-calendar that holds only availability events of a consumer. This availability sub-calendar is filled by a consumer in TeamUp Calendar. This sub-calendar is designed to help consumers and clients manage their schedules more efficiently by providing a clear overview of when they are available or when they desire a resource. If a consumer wants to enter his availability only he/she must select their calendar type to Availability. 

![][image56]

One of the key benefits of using an availability sub-calendar is that it helps to avoid scheduling conflicts. By having a clear overview of when someone is available, it becomes easier to schedule an event without having to worry about double-booking or overlapping the event.

The availability sub-calender of a consumer can be found in the scheduler calendar hierarchy.  

![][image57]

The scheduler will try to assign a resource for each consumer availability events.

2. **What is a non-availability sub-calendar?**

Non-availability sub-calendar is a type of consumer sub-calendar and only holds the non-availability events of a consumer.  This sub-calendar is specifically designed to hold the non-availability schedule of a consumer for the event and this sub-calendar is filled by the consumer itself.

As you may know, Team2Book allows consumers to schedule events and book resources according to their availability. However, there may be times when a consumer is not available for certain events or resources. In such cases, the non-availability sub-calendar can be used.

If a consumer wants to enter his Non-availability event only,  he/she must select their calendar type to Non-availability. 

![][image58]

By selecting this calendar, a consumer can enter their non-availability event for the resource they wish to book. This indicates that the consumer is unavailable at that particular time and cannot be scheduled for the event.

The Non-availability sub-calender of a consumer can be found in the scheduler calendar hierarchy.  

![][image59]

The scheduler will try to assign a resource to each consumer with a non-availability sub-calendars for each day that the consumer did not enter an unavailable event.  This might be handy for consumers usually working full-time but only want to enter the days they are on vacation.  

A notification will be sent to the Client Manager if a non-available consumer is assigned to a special event.  

Our special event assigner will not assign an unavailable consumer to a special event.

3. **What is a mixed sub-calendar?**

The Mixed sub-calendar is one of the types of consumer sub-calendar that holds both the availability and non-availability sub-calendar of the consumer. This type of sub-calendar accepts both availability and non-availability events, without any implicit availability or non-availability.

![][image60]  
To use these features, it is necessary to enable the Mixed sub-calendar in the client detail section. To do so, please follow these steps: go to the Members area, then click on the client dashboard, then select the client detail tab, and finally click on Enable Mixed Sub-calendar radio button to Yes.

**![][image61]**

In order to differentiate between availability and non-availability events in your scheduler section, there is a custom field event type that you can select.

![][image62]

This might be useful for example for doctors of medical clinics.  Doctors might first enter their Non-Availability for the walk-in clinics and then enter their availability for regular shifts.

4. **What is a working sub-calendar?**

A working sub-calendar is a type of consumer sub-calendar that is designed to hold a single consumer assigned or working event.

In other words, a working sub-calendar is a calendar that is dedicated to tracking the schedule of one specific consumer or worker.

For example, if you run a small business and have several consumers who work different shifts, you could create a separate working sub-calendar for each consumer. Each calendar would contain only the events and schedules that are relevant to that particular consumer's schedule.

If a consumer wishes to have a working calendar, it is important to enable the "include a working calendar" option in the consumer section. A consumer can also select which events to show on their working calendar. 

![][image63]

This will allow consumers to access the working sub-calendar, which can be found in the scheduler calendar hierarchy.

![][image64]

Working sub-calendars indicate when a resource is assigned to a consumer.  Availability sub-calendar lack the information about special events and because an available consumer might not always be assigned to a resource, a available consumer is not necessarily assigned a resource on a given day.  

Working sub-calendars are what is typically synched with the consumer personal agenda software (Google Calendar, Apple Calendar or Outlook).  This way, the consumer only sees its own assignments.

5. **What is a resource sub-calendar?**

A resource sub-calendar is a type of sub-calendar that is specifically designed to hold resource events. These events are related to the use of resources such as meeting rooms, equipment, or lab rooms. Resource sub-calendars are useful for organizations that need to manage their resources efficiently. By creating a separate calendar for resource events, it becomes easier to track the availability and usage of these resources. This can help prevent double bookings and ensure that resources are being used effectively. 

![][image65]

In summary, a resource sub-calendar is a specialized type of calendar that holds resource events. 

The Team2Book scheduler will attempt to assign a resource to each available consumer.

6. **What is a closed day sub-calendar?**

Closed day sub-calendar allows users to designate certain days as unavailable for scheduling. These could be holidays, weekends, or other non-working days. This type of sub-calendar is specifically designed to hold special holiday events or closed day events that are not scheduled by the scheduler. The closed day sub-calendar can be found in the Sub-calendars  Manager section and is a great way to keep track of important dates that may affect your scheduling. By using this feature, you can easily mark days as closed or unavailable without affecting your regular schedule.

![][image66]

Closed day sub-calendar is available by default.  Looking at the closed days, users can avoid marking themself available on business closed days.  

We recommend you use the closed day sub-calendar to enter special days.  If your business is always closed on some days of the week (e.g. weekends), we recommend that you enter this information here instead: Client Dashboard-\>Client details \-\> ‘Closed Time’ section of the client details.

7. **What is a free resource sub-calendar?**

A free resource sub-calendar is a specific type of sub-calendar that only holds free resource events. A free resource sub-calendar allows you to show all the free resources on a separate sub-calendar.  To make this sub-calendar available in the scheduler sub-calendar, it should be enabled in the client details section. Once enabled, it will allow you to view all free resource events in one place, making it easier to schedule appointments and manage resources.   

![][image67]

By looking at this sub-calendar, a consumer can quickly see resources that remain free and enter a last minute resource reservation.

This sub-calendar is an essential tool for managing resources and ensuring that they are utilized effectively. 

8. **How can I see which consumer is assigned to a resource?**

By going to the resource event, you can easily read which consumers are assigned to a resource. This is particularly useful when you need to quickly check who is scheduled for a specific task.

To see which consumer is assigned to a resource, you can follow these steps: 

1\. Open the Teamup Calendar  
2\. Navigate to the resources sub-calendar  which you want to check. 

![][image68]  
3\. Click on the desired resource event to select it.   
4\. In the details pane on the right-hand side, you should see the consumer assigned to the resource. 

The resource event holds important information such as the consumer name, consumer role, event type, resource, date, and hours of the schedule. For instance, you can see that A. Awoke is assigned to the Office 4  on Mon May 8, 2023, from 8:30 to 21:00.

![][image69]

9. **How can I see the working schedule of a specific consumer?**

To view the working schedule of a specific consumer, you can follow these easy steps:

1\. Go to the scheduler in the client details section.   
2\. Click on the scheduler tab.  
3\. Click on the Open in Teamup button.   
3\. Navigate to the scheduler sub-calendars.   
4\. In the Working schedule sub-calendar, you can easily hide the other consumer working schedules by a single tab or you can click on the eye icon on the right of the consumer’s working sub-calendar.    
5\. You can then view the specific consumer's working schedule.

For example, you can view E. Tamiru's working schedule by hiding the schedules of other consumer with just one click.

![][image70]

10. **How do I know which resources are free?**

To find out which resources are free, simply follow these quick and easy steps: 

First, head to the client details page and make sure that the free resources and booking options are enabled. This will allow you to view free resources on a separate sub-calendar and enable consumers to reserve those resources based on their availability. Be sure to click "save" once you've adjusted these settings. 

Next, navigate to the scheduler and run the scheduler. From there, you can click the ‘Open in Teamup button’ then go to the manager section and click on "free resources". You can hide the other sub-calendar with just one click to view the free resource events only (by clicking on the eye icon) and show only the events associated with free resources, giving you a clear view of which resources are available to book. 

**![][image71]**

11. **How do I change sub-calendars permissions?**

Every user on Team2Book has sub-calendars permission (Read-only, Modify or No Access). This means that you have the ability to manage and control who can view or modify sub-calendars and its events.

Client managers can easily change the sub-calendar permissions by following these simple steps:

1\. From the Client Dashboard, Go to the user (Consumer, Viewer, and Client Managers) and click on the update button.  
2\. Scroll down and click on the permission that is found to the right of  the sub-calendar name.  
3\. To change the sub-calendars permission, simply click on the permission field and select the appropriate option. The available permission choices are read-only, modify, and not shared (no access or no view). You can choose which permission choice  is appropriate for each sub-calendar.

Use the \+ and \- buttons to add or remove sub-calendar permissions respectively.

![][image72]

12. **Which sub-calendar permissions should I use?**

Here are the recommended sub-calendar permissions for consumers, viewers, and client managers:

For Consumers: 

Consumers are typically people who are requesting resources, such as meeting rooms or equipment. They should be granted read-only access to each resource sub-calendar(s) so that they can view availability and book resources as needed, and should be granted Modify access for their availability/ non-availability sub-calendar so that they can enter their availability and non-availability. 

![][image73]

For Viewers: 

Viewers are users who have read-only access to a Teamup resource  system. They can view the sub-calendars or the resource events, but they cannot make any changes or add any content. Viewers can be given access to specific sub-calendars or all sub-calendars in the system. 

Viewers should be granted read-only access to the relevant sub-calendar(s) or all sub-calendars in the system. 

![][image74]

For Client Managers: 

Client Managers are sometimes responsible for making sure that every consumer is entering their availability or non-availability.  They also are responsible to enter business closed days and special events.   They should be granted full access to the relevant sub-calendar(s) so that they can add or modify bookings as needed to fulfill the consumer requests.  

![][image75]

Since Team2Book is overwriting, every 15 minutes, all the events of the resources sub-calendars, regardless of the user role, the permission of the resource sub-calendars should always be set to Read-only.

13. **How do I change the sub-calendar's color?**

To change the color of the sub-calendar for your consumer and resource events, you need to follow a few simple steps. Firstly, navigate to the client details section of the client dashboard. Then, decide whether you want to change the color for a resource or a consumer event. Once you have made your selection, go to the resources or consumers page, click on the update button which will take you to a new page. From there, you will be able to easily change the color of your sub-calendar by simply clicking on the calendar color option. By following these steps, you should have no trouble in changing the sub-calendar color to your preference.  

Note: the sub-calendar color you select from the Team2Book Client Dashboard is the color you will see in Teamup Calendar.  
![][image76]

14. **Which sub-calendar’s color should I use?**

You can use any sub-calendar color from the 48 color options available in the system for your consumer or resource event. This means that you have the flexibility to choose a color that best fit your needs and makes it stand out in Teamup. Whether you prefer a bold and vibrant color or a more subtle shade, our system has a wide range of options to choose from. You can easily select your preferred color when creating or editing consumers or resources, and it will be displayed on the Teamup calendar.  

![][image77]

Here is a few strategies you can use:

1) Have a different color for each resource.  This will make it easier to see with consumer is assigned to which resource.  
   ![][image78]  
     
2) You may decide to use the same sub-calendar color for both the consumer availability sub-calendar, the consumer working sub-calendar and the reserved resource sub-calendar.  For example Dr Boutthillier availability sub-calendar, working sub-calendar and office could all be set to yellow.

3) Some clients prefer to set the same color to a consumer role.  For example, all the nurse’s sub-calendars could be set to yellow.

4) Some clients prefer to group resource sub-calendar using the same color.  For example, a client has a blue, yellow and orange section in their building.  They can decide to set the same blue color to resource sub-calendars 101-110, set the yellow color to resource sub-calendars 115-130 and so on.  
   

**Events**

1. **How do I enter an event?**

Consumers and client managers can enter events in Team2Book by following a few easy steps:

For Client managers: 

As a client manager, you can simply go to the scheduler and open Teamup Calendar by clicking on the "Open Teamup calendar" button. Once you do this, you can select the day you want to enter an event and a new pop-up page will appear. On this page, you can give a title to your event, although it is optional (Team2Book currently ignores the information you put in the description field).  Afterward, select the event time you wish to schedule and the consumer sub-calendar in the calendar custom field. Once you have done this, just click on "Save" and your event will be scheduled.

![][image79] 

For Consumer: 

As a consumer, you can easily enter events by following a quick and simple process. First, you will receive an email containing a calendar link when the client adds you as a consumer. Once you have the link, simply click on it and your calendar will open up. To add an event, click on the specific day you would like to schedule it for. This will prompt a new pop-up page to appear. On this page, you can give your event a title name (although this is optional) and select the time you would like it to occur. Once you have completed those steps, click on the 'save' button, and your event will be scheduled into your calendar with ease.

![][image80]

The steps are similar if you are entering events using the Teamup Calendar mobile app. 

Note: usually consumers only have a modify permission to their own availability or non-availability sub-calendar.  This sub-calendar should be selected by default in the calendar custom field.

2. **How do I enter a repeated event?**

To easily enter a repeated event as a client manager, open the Teamup calendar by going to the scheduler in the client dashboard. Similarly, if you are a consumer, click on your calendar link, and select the day you want to start entering the repeated event. A new pop-up page will display, and you can give your event a title name, or leave it blank as it's optional. Next, click the "Repeats" radio button, and options will pop up. You can select the event you want to enter as a daily, Mon-Fri, weekly, monthly, or yearly option. It's important to note that selecting the "daily" option includes weekends, whereas "Mon-Fri" excludes them. The weekly option lets you enter repeated events weekly, while the monthly option allows you to enter events that repeat monthly. Finally, by clicking the yearly option, you can enter events that repeat yearly.

You can also specify the repeat frequency of your event by selecting "repeat every day, week, month, year" as per your desired option. Additionally, you can select the continuing period of your event by choosing "forever, for, until" options. Lastly, be sure to click "done" and save your repeated event. With these simple steps, you can easily enter repeated events on the Team-up calendar.

![][image81]

Note: To make it easier to understand, we recommend that the ‘From and To fields’ refer to a single day.  If you need more than 1 repeated event, we recommend you create a separate events.  For example, you can create one repeated event that will occur every Monday and another repeated event that will occur every Friday.

3. **How do I delete an event?**

If you wish to delete an event from your scheduled activities, please follow these simple steps: 

If you are a client manager, please access your dashboard and proceed to the scheduler section and click the ‘Open in Teamup’ button.  From the Teamup Calendar you can select the event you want to delete. Then, simply click on the event and a new page will appear. Finally, click on the "delete" button to confirm the action. 

If you are a consumer, please click on the calendar link from any Team2Book notification to access your scheduled activities. Then, select the event you wish to delete and click on it.   
![][image82]

A new page will open, and you'll see a "delete" button that you can click on to confirm the deletion. 

![][image83]

4. **How do I delete an event from a series?**

If you have any repeated or series events that you would like to delete, please follow the steps listed below. 

If you are a client manager, open Teamup from the scheduler section of the client dashboard. If you are a consumer, click on the calendar link from any received Team2Book notification and locate the repeated or series event you want to delete. Once you've found the event, click on it to open a new page. From this page, click on the delete button. 

Please note that if you have multiple events in the series, there are options to delete. First, you can click on "single," which will apply to this event only. No other events in the series will be affected. Second, you can click on "future," which will apply to this and all following events only. Past events will be unaffected. Finally, you can click on "all events," which will apply to every event in this series. You can select any of these options as per your requirement.

![][image84]

5. Can I enter overlapping events?  
6. **How do I use the resource custom field?**

The resource custom field enables consumers to reserve specific resources. When not set the consumer’s resource preferences will be used.  To display this optional custom field, you must first enable the "Free Resources and Booking" feature in the client details.

![][image85]  
To make use of the resource custom field from Teamup Calendar, simply select the day you want to schedule an event for and click on the event. This will bring up a new pop-up page where you can find the resource custom field. Depending on your event type, you can utilize the resource field to choose an available resource and assign it to your event.

![][image86]

We usually recommend that you leave the resource field empty and use it only if you need to to reserve a free resource at the last minute.  Please also note that a consumer with higher or equal priority may already be assigned the desired resource.  Therefore a consumer may not always get assigned to the resource selected. 

7. **How do I use the event type custom field?**

The event type custom field is a consumer event type that is specially designed to offer consumers the convenience to choose to enter either an availability or a non-availability using their unique sub-calendar. It is only applicable for mixed sub-calendar consumers who would like to make use of this feature. To use the event type custom field, simply open Teamup Calendar and select the specific day that you intend to use this field. Next, click on the event and a new page will appear. Within this page, you will find the event type custom field which can be utilized as per your event type requirements.

![][image87]

You can assign a mixed sub-calendar to a given consumer from the Consumer section of the Client Dashboard.

8. **How do I use the target event custom field?**

The target event custom field is an exciting feature that allows consumers to streamline their availability or non-availabilities by shifts. With regular or special shifts, the consumer can better target their event preferences. To start using the target event custom field, you will need to enable it in the client detail dashboard.   
![][image88]

Once this has been done, simply go to the scheduler dashboard and select the day you want to use the custom field for. From there, click on the event and a new pop-up page will display. Here you will find the target event custom field, which you can customize as per your event type.

![][image89]

For example, say Dr Bouthillier is not available for a special event on May 2\.  She can then select Non-Availability for the event type and Special for the target event.  This will make sure the client manager will get notified if she tries to assign a special event to Dr Bouthillier on May 2\.

9. **What are Team2Book’s special events?**

Team2Book assigns a resource to available consumers.  The availability or non-availability events the consumers are entering are what we call regular events.

You can also use Team2Book to assign special events.  When you need to schedule a specific consumer to work at a specific time and optionally using a specific resource, then you will need to use what we call special events.  

Examples of special events:

- I need Dr Bouthillier to work on wednesday night in the emergency room.  
  - *Specific consumer to work at a specific time using  a specific resource (emergency room)*  
- I need Sarah to work at the reception desk on wednesday night.  
  - *Specific consumer to work at a specific time using a specific resource*  
- I need Dr Tremblay to work next saturday.   
  - *Specific consumer to work at a specific time but no need to use a specific resource.*

Special events have priority over regular events.  It is the client manager that assigns consumers to special shifts. To accomplish this, you can utilize the custom fields feature of the Teamup calendar. To utilize special events, you will need to subscribe to Teamup calendar pro or higher subscription plan. 

Typically, client managers will assign consumers to their designated special shifts after they have entered their availability/non-availability.

For more info [click here](https://team2book.com/category/tutorials)

10. **Can a special event type have a reserved resource?**

Yes, a special event type will have a reserved resource. However, in case the special event type doesn't have a reserved resource, the events will be assigned to resources preferred or reserved by the assigned consumer.

![][image90]

11. **How do I use the ‘Reserved Special Event Type’ custom field?**

The ‘Reserved Special Event Type’ Custom Field is designed to assist Client Managers in reserving resources for special events. 

First make sure that special events are enabled (Client Dashboard \-\> Client Details \-\> Special Events)  
![][image91]

To efficiently use the Reserved Special Event Type Custom Field, navigate to the Scheduler Dashboard and choose a date that best suits your needs. After identifying the preferred date, click on the event, and a new pop-up page will display. On this page, you will find the Reserved Special Event Type Custom Field, which you can tailor according to your event needs.

![][image92]

You will be able to choose between all the special events type you defined in the Client Dashboard \-\> Special Events Type

![][image93]

12. **How do I use the Assigned Consumer custom field?**

First select a special type using the ‘Reserved Special Event Type’ custom field.  

The ‘Reserved: Assigned Consumer’ custom field allows client managers to assign specific consumers to special events. To utilize this feature, you must first add the consumers to the list of "consumers that can be assigned to special events" in the client dashboard. Once this is done, you can easily select the desired consumer. 

This field must be used with the ‘Reserved Special Event Type’ custom field.

![][image94]

13. **What are the three ways to assign consumers to special events?**

1\. Manual Assignment: Go to Teamup calendar, select a day, and a new page will pop up. Enter the time and choose the sub-calendar ‘manager \>special’ select the ‘Reserved: Special Event type’ and ‘Reserved: target consumer’ then finally, click on "Save". 

![][image95]

2\. Automatic Assignment: This option involves enabling the automatic assignment feature in the special event dashboard.  

![][image96]

To assign consumers to special events automatically, go to the scheduler dashboard and click on "Assign Special Events". 

![][image97]

Select the special event you want to assign to consumers and enter the dates you want to assign, then click on "Assign". The scheduler will automatically assign the consumer to the special event. 

![][image98]

Using this method, you will not have to create special events manually.  Team2book will automatically assign consumers using the list of defined target consumers, their share of Events and past 90 days assignation.  
![][image99]

3\. Assign on Schedule Offset Day: Enable the "Assign on Schedule Offset Day" option in the special event type dashboard. 

![][image100]  
The scheduler will assign special events for the last scheduled month on the schedule's offset day. For example, assume the following configurations: the number of months to schedule \= 3, the schedule offset \= 7\. Now, on the 7th day of every month, the scheduler will assign special events for the month after the next (last month). You can take advantage of the schedule offset (from the client details menu) to give you enough time to perform this task. 

Team2book will always schedule the special shifts in priority over normal resource assignments. Therefore, completing the special shifts assignments before the schedule offset day is preferable but not mandatory.

![][image101]  
If using method 2 or 3 above, we recommend that you take the time to verify the automatic special event assignments done by Team2Book to make sure it fits your organization needs.

14. **How can I schedule a consumer for a special event on closed days?**

Simply head to your client details dashboard, and turn the radio button to 'yes' for the 'schedule special events on closed days' option, then click save. Once enabled, you'll be able to schedule special events on days when you're typically closed.  
**![][image102]**

15. **Do you support 2 events per day for the same consumer?**

Yes, Team2Book does support 2 events per day for the same consumer. Consumers are allowed to enter 2 events per day according to their needs.

Currently, we do not support 3 events on the same day.

16. **How can I assign multiple consumers to work at the same time?**

Sometimes, you need 2 consumers to work together using different resources.  For example, you may have 1 doctor that needs to work with a nurse assistant at the same time.

First, make sure that Linked Consumers are enabled from the Client Dashboard \-\> Client Details.

![][image103]  
Then go to Teamup Calendar and create an event for one consumer.  Then select the other consumer in the linked event field. 

![][image104]

If no resource is available for each consumer of the linked consumers event, the client manager will get notified.

Note: Linked consumers event only works for consumers with availability sub-calendar or with a mixed sub-calendar and when entering an availability event type.

**Statistics** 

1. **What type of statistics can Team2Book provide?**  
   

Team2Book offers consumer workload statistics and free resource data to help you manage your workload more effectively. By using the consumer workload pie chart, you can easily see the workload of each consumer for a selected period. The chart displays a breakdown of the shifts worked by each consumer, making it easy to see how many evening shifts were worked during a given quarter or another period. The free resource data is equally valuable, providing information about the number of days with available resources for each weekday during a selected period. For example, you can use this data to find out how many days in April had at least 3 free resources available, or how many Tuesdays had at least 2 resources available.

![][image105]

2. **Can I export the statistics?**

Yes, you can easily export the statistics of the free resource and consumer workload reports by clicking on the “download report” button at the top of the line and pie chart statistics report. This feature allows you to save and share your reports with others in PDF format or CSV format.

![][image106]  
for more info [click here](https://team2book.com/tutorials/statistics-module) 

**Troubleshooting** 

1. **How can I manually refresh the scheduling?**

To manually refresh the scheduler, 

As a client manager, you can refresh the scheduler by visiting the scheduler dashboard and clicking on the "run the scheduler" button. Once you do this, the scheduler will refresh and update the schedule accordingly. 

![][image107]

If you're a consumer, simply open your calendar link and navigate to the end of the calendar where you'll find a refresh button. Click on the "refresh" button, and your calendar will update in real-time.

![][image108]

Please remember to refresh regularly your Teamup Calendar window to see the latest results.

2. **How often is the scheduling refreshed?**

The regular scheduler will be called automatically every 15 minutes.  The regular scheduler updates all the Teamup Resources’ events.  The full scheduler is called twice a day.  The full scheduler updates all the Teamup Resources’ events and the list of scheduling problem.  

3. **How do I know if there is no more resource available for a consumer?**

To check this issue, we recommend checking the scheduler dashboard to see if any notifications are displayed below the scheduler. These notifications will indicate if there are no more resources available for a consumer scheduling problem. Please note that these notifications are only refreshed every time the full scheduler runs, which is typically every 12 hours or when you click on the “Run Full Scheduler button”.  

![][image109]  
For every new occurrence of this error, the client manager with notification enabled should be advised by email.

4. **How do I know if a consumer is not available for a special event?**

To check this issue, we recommend checking the scheduler dashboard to see if any notifications are displayed below the scheduler.  

As an example, Neway Debeb entered a non-availability event on May 2, 2023, from 8:30 to 21:00. However, the client manager assigned him to a special event during that time. The errors will be presented below the scheduler as a scheduling problem.

![][image110]

5. **Why don't I see a consumer in the target consumers list?**

Please note that the target consumer list custom field is specifically designed for the  special event. In order for a consumer to be added to the list of 'Reserved assigned consumers', you must enable the “Allow Special Event Assignment” option. To do so, simply log in to the client dashboard, select 'consumer', and click 'update' for the desired consumer. From there, enable 'allow special event assignment' by selecting 'yes' in the radio button.

![][image111]

Once enabled, the consumer's name will be added to the target consumer list.  

*Please note that you will need to hard reset your Teamup page (or log out and log back in) to see the changes from Teamup or run the scheduler to see the changes from the Client Dashboard.*  
![][image112]

6. **Why do I get a consumer not available on closed day notification?**

The scheduler will continue to schedule availability events on closed days. However, if you do not wish to receive notifications related to unavailable consumers on closed days, you may simply go to the client details section and disable the "verify consumer availability on closed days" custom field. Once disabled, the managers will not receive any notifications related to the unavailability of consumers who are assigned to special events on closed days.  
![][image113]

7. **Why is the consumer not getting his preferred resource?**

The scheduler assigns the available resources based on consumer priority.  This way it ensures that high priority consumers get reserved resources first. The priority number ranges from 1 to 10 and when a consumer has priority 1, it means that they have a high priority and a reserved resource. Consumers with priority 2 to 10 have no reserved resource, but they do have resource preferences. As a client manager, you can assign priority numbers to your consumers as per your need. Priority 1 consumers will be assigned a resource first, followed by priority 2 consumers, and so on. If you choose a priority other than 1 (between 2 to 10), the resource preference field will display. The resource will be assigned based on their resource preference. In the resource preference field, you can used each preference number once and you cannot choose the same resource name more than once. 

However, a consumer will not get their preferred resource if another consumer's priority or preferences is higher than theirs. This ensures that all consumers' preferences are taken into account and resources are assigned in a fair manner.  For the same priority, the scheduler will start assigning resources in a random manner.  For example, if John and Peter both have a priority 2 and both have the same resource preferences, the scheduler might decide to give the highest resource preferences (1)  to John, Peter will then get his next preferred resource.

8. **Why is the consumer not getting his preferred resource and the resource remains available?**

It is important to note that a consumer may not receive their preferred resource even if it is available, if the scheduled resource time and consumer availability time do not align. For instance, if a consumer is available all day but the resource is only accessible in the morning due to the opening and closing hours of the resource, the consumer will not receive their preferred resource as their availability time does not match the resource schedule time.  

The scheduler first tries to provide a free for the whole day resource to the consumers.  This design prevent resource conflicts between a consumer that does not finish on time and the next consumer scheduled to use the same resource on the same day.

9. **When do I receive notifications?**

Please note that notifications can be turned off or partially turned off by your team manager.
