If you are added as a Team2Book member, you will receive a welcome notification. If you are a consumer, and notifications are enabled, you will also receive a reminder to enter your availability / non-availability (on the same days each month as configured by the client manager). 

If you are a client manager, you will receive notifications regarding scheduling problems or when they are resolved. And if you are a principal client manager, you will receive Team2Book account management (billing) notifications.

Here is an incomplete list of notifications currently being sent by Team2Book.  Other notifications type are regularly added.

* Welcome email  
* Password reset   
* Enter availability / non-availability  
* Reminder to enter availability / non-availability  
* Unavailable consumer assigned  
* No resource left  
* Insufficient number of consumers  
* Invalid email addres  
* Last minute changes  
* Team2book account management notifications (principal client manager only)  
* Scheduler as completed  
* Event partially scheduled  
* Scheduling Problem solved  
* Incomplete mixed event  
* Overelapping Mixte Events   
* Ignored custom fields value


If you are a consumer, your Team Manager may still decide to turn on some of the notification types above. 

10. I received a password with my welcome email but entering this password on my mobile does not work.  Why?

**Advanced**

1. **How can I sync my workday sub-calendar with my personal agenda?**

If you're looking to sync your Workday sub-calendar with your personal agenda, it's actually quite simple. Firstly, open Teamup on a computer using your access link. Then, click on the hamburger button (the triple bar ≡ or trigram symbol ☰) located at the right end of the calendar. This will bring up some options. Hover over and select “Preference”. From the new menu that drops down, hover over and select “iCalendar Feeds”. 

**![][image114]**

Next, you'll be taken to a new page. 

![][image115]

These calendar feeds allow you to view all your calendar data from Teamup in other popular calendar products, such as Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, Outlook and many others. It's important to note that these feeds are read-only. This means that you can view all your calendar data in your external calendar but you won't be able to make any changes from there. Here, you'll find the web addresses for each sub-calendars for which you have access.

When you hover over the sub-calendar with your mouse, you will see sub-calendar descriptions with the full names of the sub-calendar popping up. Each sub-calendar will show you a feed (Web Address). For example, when you hover over the first calendar, you will see that it's for Consumer A. Awoke, whose role is Doctor. and their calendar type is “Avail”. This will allow you to easily navigate through the different calendars and find the right sub-calendar you need.

![][image116]

Find your **Working sub-calendar** (not your Availability sub-calendar) and copy the web address which corresponds to your Working sub-calendar. Usually, the sub-calendar name is “Consumers \> Working \> Your Name”.  Finally, you can then paste the web address link into your preferred personal agenda (Google Calendar, Outlook, iCal and others.) to sync the two.  Follow the instructions of your personal agenda on how to add external iCal feed. 

2. **What is your office rooms (resources) rotation algorithm?**

Office rotation allows organization to increase the chances that a given priority 2 to 10 consumer does not always get assigned the same resource.

Our resource/office rotation algorithm is as follows: The rotation direction is left, meaning that the second resource preference becomes the first and the first resource preference becomes the last preference.

We have four options:

* No Rotation: Resource preferences will not change. (default)  
* Per Day: Resource preferences orders will be rotated on each day of the week. On the same day of the week, the resource preferences order is similar. For example, the consumer will have similar resource preferences every Monday, Tuesday, etc.  
* Per Week: Resource preferences order will be rotated on each week of a month.  
* Per Month: Resource preferences orders will be rotated on each month of the year.

![][image117]

**Special events Assigner**

1. **What is the special events assigner and how do I enable it?**

The special events assigner is a highly-efficient tool that eliminates the hassle of manually assigning consumers to special events. The special events assigner automatically assigns consumers to the special event (nights or weekend shifts, emergency clinics, on-call duties, etc.). 

By logging in to your client dashboard and clicking on the special event type, you can create a new special event or update an existing one using the update button from the table. Once you have done that, simply click the enable automatic assignment radio button to activate this feature. With the automatic assignment feature enabled, consumers will be assigned to special events automatically, ensuring a seamless and hassle-free experience for all involved.

![][image118]

Team2Book will attempt to fairly distribute special events for the next month to schedule based on the last 3 month assignments.  Please note this tool is provided to ease your work but we do recommend that you take a look at the automatic assignment to make sure it fits your needs.  

There are cases where an even number of assignments is not desired.  For example, when a new consumer joins the organization or when a consumer returns after a long-term leave.  Team2Book might detect that this consumer did not perform any special event shifts in the past 3 months and attempt to correct this by assigning the newly arrived consumer to many upcoming special events.

2. **When will the special event assigner run?**

To run the special event assignment, you have two options. The first option is to go to the scheduler dashboard and manually click on "assign special events". The second option is to go to the special event type dashboard and enable "assign on the schedule offset day". When you enable this feature, the scheduler will automatically assign special events for the last scheduling month on the scheduled offset day.

For instance, if the number of months to schedule is three, and the schedule offset is seven, then on the 6th day of every month (offset day \-1), the scheduler will assign special events for the month after the next (last month). For Exaample, if today we are in January, on the 6 of January, the assigner will assign special events for the month of March.  This feature is particularly useful save time by automatically assign consumers to spécial events in a fair way.  ![][image119]

3. **How can I select which consumers can get assigned to one or more special events?**

To select which consumers to get assigned to a special event, you can follow these steps: 

1. Go to the Client Dashboard.   
2. Go to Consumers  
3. Click the update button beside the desired consumer.  
4. Select ‘Yes’ to the option ‘Allow Special Event Assignment’ and submit.  
5. Repeat for all the consumers you want to be available for special event assignments.

![][image111]  
One done, the consumer’s name will now show up as a choice for the Teamup custom field “Reserved : Assigned Consumer”.  Their name will also show in the list of consumers that can be assigned to a spécial event type.

4. **How do I select the day of the week the assigner will assign a consumer?**

To easily select the day of the week for the consumer assigner in Team2Book, simply navigate to the special event type section of the dashboard and click the "update" button for the desired special event. Once you do this, a new page will appear where you can see the "automatic assignment configuration" custom field. From here, you can indicate the frequency of the event, including which week you want it to repeat, the day of the week, and the time you want it to occur. By default, the value is set to 1, indicating that the event will repeat every week. If you want it to repeat every month instead, simply set the value to 4\. It's that simple\!  
![][image120]

5. **How do you decide who is assigned to a special event?**

To assign someone to a special event, simply access the Dashboard \-\> Special events and select the desired special event. Once you have selected the event, click on the "Update" button and a new page will appear. On this page, you will find a "Target Consumer" custom field where you can make your selection. 

![][image121]

To add a consumer to the target list, simply click on the green "plus" button and select the consumer from the list. This will assign the consumer to the special event. If you need to remove a consumer from the target list, simply click on the red "minus" button and select the consumer you wish to remove. 

With this simple process, you can easily decide who is assigned to a special event and manage your target consumer list with ease.

6. **How do I change who is assigned to a special event?**

If you need to change the consumers who is assigned to a special event, it's a simple process that can be done on the special event type dashboard table. Begin by selecting the special event type you want to update, then click the update button. A new page will pop up, and from here there is a “target consumer” custom field. Click the option on the assigned name of the consumer, and a list of all the consumer names will pop up. From the list, you can easily hover over and click the name of the new consumer you want to assign.

 For instance, if you want to change the consumer from E. Tamiru, you can click their name from the target consumer's list. The list of other consumer names will then pop up, and you can easily hover and select the name of the new consumer you want to assign.

![][image122]

7. **Why does the special events assigner erase existing special events?**

You have 2 options to run the assigner.  One option is by pressing the ‘Assign Special Events’ button from the scheduler section of the Client Dashboard.  A popup will open and you can select the date range to assign consumers to special events.  You can also specify the Special Event Type.  Any special events between the selected From and To date will be erased and replaced with new special event assignments.

![][image123]

The second option is when you activated the ‘Assign On the Schedule Offset Day’ option.  This option can be found in Client Dashboard \-\> Special Event Type \-\> Update.  ![][image124]

On the schedule offset day, the assigner will erase all the special event assignments for the next month to schedule.  The assigner will not erase the existing specials events for the preceding months.

8. **How can I tell the scheduler to assign more or less special events to some consumers?**

By utilizing the "share of events (%)" feature, you can easily tailor the distribution of special events to specific consumers. All you need to do is indicate the relative amount or share of the special events for each target consumer. The default setting is that every target consumer receives a 100% share of events, guaranteeing that special events are distributed fairly among them. 

For instance, if a target consumer has a 200% share of events, that consumer will be allocated twice the number of events compared to consumers with a 100% share of events. Similarly, if a target consumer has a 50% share of events, that consumer will receive half the number of events than consumers with a 100% share of events.

![][image125]
